# Land Of Festivals — Indian Festivals Deep Dive

## About This Site
A research-based guide to Indian festivals, their regional variations, historical origins, current practices, and diaspora celebrations worldwide. Built from public domain primary sources, academic research, and community documentation.

## Key Pages
- Homepage: https://indianfestivals.net/
- All Festivals: https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/
- About & Methodology: https://indianfestivals.net/about.html

## Major Festivals
- [Diwali](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/diwali.html) — Festival of Lights (Hindu, Jain, Sikh)
- [Holi](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/holi.html) — Spring festival of colors
- [Durga Puja / Navaratri / Dussehra](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/durga-puja-navaratri-dussehra.html) — Nine-night goddess festival
- [Eid-ul-Fitr & Eid-ul-Adha](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/eid-ul-fitr-eid-ul-adha.html) — Islamic festivals in India
- [Ganesh Chaturthi](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/ganesh-chaturthi.html) — Elephant-headed god's festival
- [Krishna Janmashtami](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/krishna-janmashtami.html) — Krishna's birth celebration
- [Maha Shivaratri](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/maha-shivaratri.html) — Night of Shiva
- [Makar Sankranti / Pongal](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/makar-sankranti-pongal.html) — Solar harvest festival
- [Onam](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/onam.html) — Kerala's harvest festival

## More Festivals
- [Raksha Bandhan](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/raksha-bandhan.html)
- [Chhath Puja](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/chhath-puja.html)
- [Baisakhi / Vaisakhi](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/baisakhi-vaisakhi.html)
- [Gurpurab / Guru Nanak Jayanti](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/gurpurab-guru-nanak-jayanti.html)
- [Mahavir Jayanti](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/mahavir-jayanti.html)
- [Buddha Purnima](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/buddha-purnima.html)
- [Christmas in India](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/christmas-in-india.html)
- [Eid-e-Milad-un-Nabi](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/eid-e-milad-un-nabi.html)
- [Nowruz / Jamshedi Navroz](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/nowruz-jamshedi-navroz.html)
- [Hornbill Festival](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/hornbill-festival.html)
- [Sarhul & Karma](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/sarhul-karma.html)
- [Bonalu & Bathukamma](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/bonalu-bathukamma.html)
- [Rath Yatra](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/rath-yatra.html) — Jagannath Puri chariot festival
- [Kumbh Mela](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/kumbh-mela.html) — World's largest pilgrimage
- [Muharram & Ashura](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/muharram-ashura.html) — Shia commemoration
- [Indian New Year](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/indian-new-year.html) — Ugadi, Gudi Padwa, Vishu, Puthandu
- [Rongali Bihu](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/rongali-bihu.html) — Assamese spring New Year
- [Easter in India](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/easter-in-india.html) — Christian Holy Week
- [Theyyam](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/theyyam.html) — Ritual dance of Kerala
- [Karva Chauth & Teej](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/karva-chauth-teej.html) — Women's fasting festivals
- [Losar](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/losar.html) — Tibetan Buddhist New Year
- [Wangala & Chapchar Kut](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/wangala-chapchar-kut.html) — Northeast indigenous
- [Republic & Independence Day](https://indianfestivals.net/festivals/republic-day-independence-day.html) — National festivals

## Research Framework
This site is built from a comprehensive research blueprint that covers:
- 34 festivals across Hindu, Muslim, Sikh, Jain, Buddhist, Christian, Zoroastrian, Adivasi, and secular traditions
- Regional variations across 28+ Indian states
- Primary source documentation (texts from 7th century BCE to 1908 CE)
- Diaspora celebrations across 6 distinct migration types
- Historical evolution through 6 layers (ancient to digital era)

## Source Policy
All primary source quotations are from PUBLIC DOMAIN texts (pre-1928). See the [full bibliography](/festival_data/10_master_source_url_index.tsv). Modern academic sources are cited under fair use.

## Ethics
This project follows strict anti-exoticizing, anti-romanticizing guidelines. We distinguish scripture, folklore, popular belief, and observed practice. Disputed histories are flagged, not smoothed over.
